verses.text: Dare any go to law 'before the unjust, and not before the saints'? the saints shall judge the world. Misplaced suits. Believers should not drag each other before pagan courts.
Dare any go to law 'before the unjust, and not before the saints'? the saints shall judge the world. Misplaced suits. Believers should not drag each other before pagan courts.
Dare any go to law 'before the unjust, and not before the saints'? the saints shall judge the world. Misplaced suits. Believers should not drag each other before pagan courts.
Is there not a wise man among you to judge? Brother goes to law with brother. A shame. Such quarrels disgrace the church.
Is there not a wise man among you to judge? Brother goes to law with brother. A shame. Such quarrels disgrace the church.
Is there not a wise man among you to judge? Brother goes to law with brother. A shame. Such quarrels disgrace the church.
It is a fault to go to law; 'why do ye not rather take wrong?' Yet ye defraud. Better to suffer. Love would rather bear loss than wrangle.
It is a fault to go to law; 'why do ye not rather take wrong?' Yet ye defraud. Better to suffer. Love would rather bear loss than wrangle.
The unrighteous shall not inherit the kingdom; 'such were some of you: but ye are washed... sanctified... justified.' Changed. Grace had made them new.
The unrighteous shall not inherit the kingdom; 'such were some of you: but ye are washed... sanctified... justified.' Changed. Grace had made them new.
The unrighteous shall not inherit the kingdom; 'such were some of you: but ye are washed... sanctified... justified.' Changed. Grace had made them new.
'All things are lawful... but all things are not expedient'; the body is 'for the Lord.' Wise liberty. Freedom is governed by what helps and honours God.
'All things are lawful... but all things are not expedient'; the body is 'for the Lord.' Wise liberty. Freedom is governed by what helps and honours God.
'All things are lawful... but all things are not expedient'; the body is 'for the Lord.' Wise liberty. Freedom is governed by what helps and honours God.
Your bodies are 'the members of Christ'; he that is joined to the Lord 'is one spirit.' United to Christ. The body too belongs to Him.
Your bodies are 'the members of Christ'; he that is joined to the Lord 'is one spirit.' United to Christ. The body too belongs to Him.
Your bodies are 'the members of Christ'; he that is joined to the Lord 'is one spirit.' United to Christ. The body too belongs to Him.
Flee fornication; 'your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost'; 'ye are bought with a price.' Glorify God. The redeemed body is God's, to be kept holy.
